text.content: Add a deterministic unit test proving a fresh SecureStore recovery marker wins when server resume target and overdue review target are also present. Branches to cover or cite: fresh recovery plus resume plus overdue collision; stale recovery ignored; resume beats overdue; overdue-only fallback; parked native cold-start path. Expected files: apps/mobile/src/components/home/LearnerScreen.test.tsx, apps/mobile/src/lib/session-recovery.test.ts, resume-crash-recovery.yaml for the native boundary, and the flow docs. Keep/document the native cold-start E2E boundary, then update CC-05 with evidence and final status.

text.content: Align LEARN-50 docs/status with current code and add/cite deterministic mobile-surface proof. Docs must state that Challenge Round is API-flag-gated by CHALLENGE_ROUND_RUNTIME_ENABLED=false, mobile renders only typed server fields, and no flag flip is in scope. Correct stale drafted-note wording: current server finalization calls validateNoteDraft() before emitting a drafted note and falls back when grounding fails. Evidence must cover offer accept/decline/don't-ask, active banner, drafted-note review/save/skip, typed SSE/done payload handling, schema grounding requirements, answer-event validation, conservative mastery decision, needs-deepening persistence, and concept-capture remaining parked/gated off. Variants: flag off vs server-emitted typed fields; offered/active/drafting/complete/declined; all-solid vs partial/missing/misconception; invalid answerEventId; grounded vs ungrounded note draft; concept-capture disabled. Expected files include flow docs, mobile session/SSE tests, challenge component tests, session-exchange tests, challenge-round service tests, and schemas tests. This is current-state proof/docs only, not the learner-pull Challenge Round redesign.

text.content: Harden HOMEWORK-08/09 regression coverage without changing product behavior unless tests expose a real gap. HOMEWORK-08 tests cover image-read failure and timeout: visible learner-facing system message, first homework stream proceeds text-only, no imageBase64/imageMimeType/imageUri leaks, and analytics emits homework_image_attach_dropped with reason, captureSource, and hasOcrText. HOMEWORK-09 tests cover result-phase subject resolution branches: confident classify shows auto-detected subject plus Change; Change opens picker; suggestedSubjectName with zero enrolled subjects auto-creates and routes to session; ambiguous candidates show candidates/enrolled/create/manual options; loading state always exposes Retake; fallback card remains actionable. Reclassification coverage proves a second image/text actually triggers a second /subjects/classify call, replacing the weak '> 0 calls' assertion. Existing homework image sandbox and pending subject-resolution image preservation tests remain passing. Run targeted mobile tests for changed files; run API subject/schema tests only if API/schema code changes.

Add helper/unit coverage proving ACCOUNT-38 switch eligibility: visible only for an active 18+ profile sharing the account with at least one minor, hidden for active minors, solo adults, and adults with no minor sibling. Branches to cover: requested-consent waiting gate; subject preview; coaching preview; switch-eligible adult; switch-ineligible active child; switch-ineligible adult with no minor sibling. Expected files include ConsentPendingGate, consent-gate-helpers, j23 consent states spec, possible test-seed additions, and flow-plan rows. Update the flow plan rows with evidence and final status.

text.content: P0 flow gap for subject creation: the PRD-defined subject-limit recovery can disappear when API copy or localization changes because mobile keys off message regex instead of a stable code, leaving capped users on a generic create-subject error without the manage/delete recovery CTA.

rich_text.plain_text: P0 flow gap for subject creation: the PRD-defined subject-limit recovery can disappear when API copy or localization changes because mobile keys off message regex instead of a stable code, leaving capped users on a generic create-subject error without the manage/delete recovery CTA.

text.content: Root cause: SUBJECT-20 is caused by create-subject recovery being coupled to English API error text. Mobile sets isSubjectLimitError by regexing err.message because POST /v1/subjects has no stable SUBJECT_LIMIT_EXCEEDED API code. Add a shared ERROR_CODES.SUBJECT_LIMIT_EXCEEDED contract and route the blocking subject-limit case through it, recommended as HTTP 409 Conflict unless the existing gate is proven subscription/payment-owned. Mobile create-subject must branch only on the typed code, not on err.message or formatted error text. Regression tests must fail on the old regex behavior: API/route coverage asserts the limit case returns { code: 'SUBJECT_LIMIT_EXCEEDED', message: ... }; mobile coverage asserts the typed code shows delete-first guidance plus manage-subjects-button; mobile negative coverage asserts a non-limit error whose message contains 'subject limit' or 'too many subjects' does not show the recovery UI. Existing create-subject success branches, non-limit errors, and Manage navigation behavior remain unchanged. Verify where subject-limit enforcement actually lives before coding; if no blocking limit gate exists, add the minimal hard-limit service gate per PRD or split soft-limit prompt/override UX into a separate WI.

text.content: P0 flow gap for owner home consent-state messaging: after the last child is archived or deleted, post-grace consent archive/delete notices can be hidden by demo fallback substitution, so the owner may miss required retention/deletion status messaging and the notice may remain unseen. No evidence of data exposure or consent gate bypass; blast radius is mobile dashboard rendering and acknowledgement visibility.

rich_text.plain_text: P0 flow gap for owner home consent-state messaging: after the last child is archived or deleted, post-grace consent archive/delete notices can be hidden by demo fallback substitution, so the owner may miss required retention/deletion status messaging and the notice may remain unseen. No evidence of data exposure or consent gate bypass; blast radius is mobile dashboard rendering and acknowledgement visibility.

text.content: Root cause: useDashboard replaces any real dashboard where children.length === 0 with /dashboard/demo, and demo data has no pendingNotices, so Home never sees dashboard.pendingNotices[0]. Regression test required: add a red/green mobile hook test proving that GET /dashboard with children: [], demoMode:false, and pendingNotices:[...] is returned unchanged and does not get replaced by GET /dashboard/demo. Keep/update the existing empty-children/no-notices test so children: [] with pendingNotices: [] may still use demo fallback. Regression test required: add/update a HomeScreen test with a valid pendingNotice shape proving the owner sees post-grace-notice-toast and the non-proxy owner acknowledgement timer calls POST /notices/:id/seen. Variants covered: empty children plus pending notices preserves the real dashboard and shows the owner toast; empty children with no pending notices keeps existing demo fallback; proxy mode still does not acknowledge the notice on the child's behalf; both consent_archived and consent_deleted pending notice types remain supported.
